hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — 2, 6, 2. You bet. Your opponent raised you back.

wrongReasons: A 2 plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that A 2 doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
3 3 makes a full house here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
With only one pair, J K wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 10 2

Your opponent flopped three of a kind and raised the flop immediately to build the pot. Sets are too strong to slow-play — they pushed their advantage on every street.
